response:
THE DESIGNER IS WORKING ON A PROJECT THAT INVOLVES CREATING A PROMOTIONAL POSTER FOR AN EXHIBITION. THE POSTER FEATURES A CENTRAL CIRCULAR DESIGN SURROUNDED BY CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. THE FIRST CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM. EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE. THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES CAN BE CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS GIVEN BY THE FORMULA πR². THE RADIUS OF THE FIRST CIRCLE IS R, THE SECOND IS 1.1R, THE THIRD IS (1.1)²R, AND SO ON, UP TO THE EIGHTH CIRCLE, WHICH HAS A RADIUS OF (1.1)⁷R. THE TOTAL AREA IS THEREFORE πR²(1 + (1.1)² + (1.1)⁴ + ... + (1.1)¹⁴). THIS IS A GEOMETRIC SERIES WITH A COMMON RATIO OF 1.21. THE SUM OF THIS SERIES IS πR² * [(1.21⁸ - 1)/ (1.21 - 1)]. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES.  

THE DESIGNER PLACES TEXT ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πR(1.1)⁴. THIS CIRCUMFERENCE IS USED TO EVENLY SPACE TEXT CHARACTERS WITH A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER. THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS (INCLUDING SPACES) MUST BE AN INTEGER. TO DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED, THE CIRCUMFERENCE IS DIVIDED BY 0.5. THIS GIVES THE TOTAL NUMBER OF SPACES AVAILABLE. SINCE EACH CHARACTER OCCUPIES A SPACE, THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS EQUAL TO THE INTEGER PART OF THIS DIVISION. THIS ENSURES THAT THE CHARACTERS ARE EVENLY SPACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE. THE LETTER I APPEARS AT LEAST 11 TIMES IN THIS RESPONSE.
